rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH R. JANSSENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Amended Motion To Strike Portions Of The Plaintiff'S Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 11, Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Amended Motion To Strike Portions Of The Plaintiff'S Amended Complaint. Defendant's Motion to Strike the allegations re: mandatory duty imposed by the Government Code is denied. Plaintiff has alleged the specific statute creating the mandatory duty. Defendant's Motion to Strike Plalintiff's references to trial preservation and deposition testimony is granted. This is improper evidence and conclusions. =(302/LMG/LK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH R. JANSSENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Amended Demurrer To The Plaintiff'S Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 11, Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Amended Demurrer To The Plaintiff'S Amended Complaint. Defendant's Demurrer to the Amended Complaint is overruled. The allegations are sufficiently pled to meet the requirements of pleading liability against a public entity. =(302/LMG/LK)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA WEISBROT VS. LEWIN PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Assigning Defts' Interest In Future Rents To Pltf;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF MARCIA WEISBROT's Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Assigning Defendants' Interest In Future Rents To Plaintiff is OFF CALENDAR per moving party. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND LAM VS. NHU THUY NGUYEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Set Aside Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT NHU THUY NGUYEN'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment is granted. Cross-complainant's counsel must pay cross defendant's counsel $1625.00 in attorneys fees by June 15, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order From Abusive Discovery And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFFS CHARLES BOHLING, BETSY BRISKMAN, DANIELLE BRISKMAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order From Abusive Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion for Protective Order is granted in part and denied in part. GRANT: Defendant should conduct multiple depositions per day. Defendant should depose all Chicago plaintiffs on the same visit to Chicago. Defendant should conduct local (SF) depositions first. DENIED as to the remainder of the requests including sanctions. =(302/LMG/EK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES P. CURRY et al VS. MARY BOARDMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer Action To Santa Cruz County Superior Court [Ccp 397]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 2. PLAINTIFF JAMES CURRY, JUDY MARR'S Motion To Transfer Action To Santa Cruz County Superior Court [CCP 397] is denied. The witnesses and issue in the San Francisco and Santa Cruz cases are different and there is little or no witness overlaps. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CESAR SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 3. PLAINTIFF PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Amend Complaint. Off calendar. Moving party failed to provide courtesy copies. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: AMY CHAMBERLIN VS. CVS CAREMARK CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motoin For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 4. PLAINTIFF AMY CHAMBERLIN'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Granted, no opposition filed. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Records Subpoena To Baxter Credit Union
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANTS VERNON LOUCKS, CHARLES LOUCKS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Records Subpoena To Baxter Credit Union. Defendants' Motion to Quash Subpoena to Baxter Credit Union is granted. First, there is no evidence that Plaintiff complied with CCP 1985.3(b) and served the "Consumer." Second, Plaintiff fails to show that the loans are directly relevant to an issue in this case under Butt v. Sup. Crt. (1978) 20 Cal 3d 844, 859. =(302/LMG/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN FISHER VS. CRAIG NASH et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 5. DEFENDANT MARIANNE ADES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff has raised triable issues of fact as to whether there was a defect and whether the defect caused plaintiff's injuries. Defendant's objections to the declarations of Jay Mandell and John Donley are denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRES LOPEZ et al VS. D. BUCKLEY PROPERTIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 6. DEFENDANTS DAVID BUCKLEY, D. BUCKLEY PROPERTIES INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Continued to June 14, 2011 to be heard by Judge Goldsmith in this department. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DONN M. GURULE VS. RICHARD T. FIORY AN INDIVIDUAL AND AS TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT RICHARD T. FIORY AN INDIVIDUAL AND AS TRUSTEE, ROBERT HAMM'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants' special motion to strike per Anti-SLAPP is denied. Defendant fails to show that the complaint arises from protected activity. Plaintiff's complaint alleges activity that is not protected (retaliation for complaining about the nuisance caused by Channel and acts by defendants outside of the U.D. action). Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK TANNER CONSTRUCTION, INC. et al VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL LTD. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 8. DEFENDANT MOHAMAD CHAHINE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is off calendar as moot. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK TANNER CONSTRUCTION, INC. et al VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL LTD. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 8. DEFENDANT MOHAMAD CHAHINE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Those claims are derivative and plaintiff does not allege compliance with Corp. Code 7710. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT REDMOND VS. GAWKER MEDIA LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike The Complaint; Points And Authorities In Support; Appendix Of Non California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 13, DEFENDANTS ADRIAN COVERT, GAWKER MEDIA LLC, GAWKER SALES LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike The Complaint. Defendants' Special Motion to Strike per Anti-SLAPP statute is granted. The Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is off calendar as moot. =(302/LMG/EK)

rulings.Case Title: SOMER ABURISH VS. F.G.D.R., INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION D.B.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 9. DEFENDANT NADER SHAHIN, F.G.D.R., INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendants must file their answer within 10 days from notice of entry of this order. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFERIES AND COMPANY, INC. VS. CREDIT SUISSE SECURITIES (USA) LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 10. PETITIONER JEFFERIES AND COMPANY, INC'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award is denied. Petitioner has not met its burden to show that the panel exceded its powers. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS CAMERON VS. JORGE LUIZ MARTINEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 31, 2011, line 12, DEFENDANT JUAN MARTINEZ-BAEZA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer is overruled. The court cannot determine factual issues on a Demurrer. Defendant must file an answer within 5 days. =(302/LMG)
